results.title: Royal children
results.brief_description: This interesting newsreel footage gives a picture of the lives of three of the British Royal Children: His Royal Highness, the Prince of Wales, Her Royal Highness Princess Anne and the Duke of York, Prince Andrew as a young child. Includes the christening photographs of Prince Charles and Princess Anne; their trips on the Royal yacht to Malta and Gibraltar; Prince Charles at school, family and national occasions.

results.title: Royal Australian Survey Corps
results.brief_description: Shows the work of the men and women of the Australian Army Survey Corps in accurately mapping Australia and Pacific areas. Focuses on the important role of aerial photography, and the follow-up from the photographs - tracing copies and finally printing maps.

results.title: Royal Australian Artillery
results.brief_description: The artillery man of today has accurate and efficient guns, unlike the men who manned the guns used to defend the colony of Sydney last century. This film describes the work and equipment of modern artillery-men in peace and wartime.

results.title: Royal Air Force police dog training
results.creator_credit: William Hammond, William Weedon and British Films
results.brief_description: Dogs can be invaluable for guarding aeroplanes, supplies and prisoners, and realising this, the RAF set up a Police Dog Training Centre. This film shows each stage in the training of one of these dogs.

results.title: Rowing for beginners
results.brief_description: The fundamentals of rowing are described and illustrated, often in slow motion. Tub pairs and eights from Magdalen College, Oxford University, at different stages of rowing experience, are photographed in action. Their good points and their faults are indicated.

results.title: Routes du soleil
results.creator_credit: Max Sautet and French Tourist Office
results.brief_description: Presents a colorful tour of Provence in the South of France, by two tourists, complete with camera and pet duck. Shows both the historical and contemporary scene, accompanied by appropriate music and commentary with well-timed humour enlivening the beautiful scenery.
results.description: Presents a colorful tour of Provence in the South of France, by two tourists, complete with camera and pet duck. Shows both the historical and contemporary scene, accompanied by appropriate music and commentary with well-timed humour enlivening the beautiful scenery.

results.title: Round the world by air
results.brief_description: A film record of the delivery flight of the first Convair Aircraft purchased by Trans-Australia Airlines. The aircraft travelled 18,000 miles in 72 hours on its round-the-world trip, passing over 25 countries on its flight from San Diego, USA, to Melbourne. Photography by Geoffrey Thompson.
results.description: A film record of the delivery flight of the first Convair Aircraft purchased by Trans-Australia Airlines. results.title: Round the twist Series 3 - Volume 5 [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Esben Storm, Pino Amenta, Ray Boseley, Bernadette O'Mahony, Patricia Edgar and Australian Children's Television Foundation
results.brief_description: The Twist family - 13-year-old twins Pete and Linda, their 8-year-old brother Bronson and their single Dad, Tony - move to an old lighthouse on the Australian coast. Here they encounter some unusual characters and strange happenings in a series of weird and comic adventures. Each episode is a self-contained story with a surprise ending, guaranteed to send you “round the twist”. In “Mali Boo”, Tony discovers an old Malibu surfboard in the garage, and Pete is keen to make a splash at the Port Niranda surf carnival, that is until the board’s real owner shows up. In “Brainless”, Bronson teams up with resident geek Anthony for a science project that ends up with Pete and Linda having their brains sucked out their noses. While Bronson is searching for their brains at the local tip, Linda and Pete’s bodies become Gribbles’ personal slaves. In “Toy Love”, Linda’s old Veronique doll comes back to haunt her, refusing to leave her alone, and neither will Gribble who has heard Linda read from the Viking Book of Love. In “The Tears Of Innocence”, Port Niranda is in the middle of a drought when days before the annual flower show, Derek the rainmaker arrives. In “The Ice Cream Man Cometh”, Linda is sacked from Mr. Gribble’s ice cream parlor, so she finds work with Paulo the master ice cream maker. In “If The Walls Could Talk”, Bronson feels left out because no-one ever tells him anything, thinking he is too young. If only the walls could talk he sighs, and then they begin to - the challenge is to make them be quiet again. In “The Big Rock”, Gribbles gets his hands on the Viking Book of Love and reads the final poem, accidentally unleashing a curse which turns Faye into a frog. Based on the books by Paul Jennings.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Esben Storm, Pino Amenta, Ray Boseley, Bernadette O’Mahony, Patricia Edgar and Australian Children’s Television Foundation
